12.reason: [Reading input into s+1 leaves s[0] uninitialized; this causes undefined behavior when accessing s[i-1] for i=1 (i.e., s[0]) which is not part of the input string.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Reading input into s+1 leaves s[0] uninitialized; this causes undefined behavior when accessing s[i-1] for i=1 (i.e., s[0]) which is not part of the input string.]

15.reason: [Accessing s[i+1] when i equals strlen(s+1) leads to out-of-bounds read (buffer overread), because valid indices are from 1 to strlen(s+1), and s[i+1] would be beyond the null terminator.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Accessing s[i+1] when i equals strlen(s+1) leads to out-of-bounds read (buffer overread), because valid indices are from 1 to strlen(s+1), and s[i+1] would be beyond the null terminator.]

25.reason: [This loop assumes that the marked segment starts at a character to expand and ends at the upper bound, but the marking logic only marks three positions (i-1, i, i+1) for a single '-', so for consecutive expansions or complex cases, this logic fails. Also, if s[i] > s[pos-1], the loop condition fails or becomes infinite due to char signedness.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop assumes that the marked segment starts at a character to expand and ends at the upper bound, but the marking logic only marks three positions (i-1, i, i+1) for a single '-', so for consecutive expansions or complex cases, this logic fails. Also, if s[i] > s[pos-1], the loop condition fails or becomes infinite due to char signedness.]
